    <item>
      <title>I Tried to Sell My House With A.I.</title>
      <link>https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/technology/sell-house-with-ai-no-realtor.html</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/technology/sell-house-with-ai-no-realtor.html</guid>
      <atom:link href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/technology/sell-house-with-ai-no-realtor.html" rel="standout"></atom:link>
      <description>Over five frantic days, I gambled my family’s life savings on a hunch that A.I. could outperform a real estate agent.</description>
      <dc:creator>Stuart A. Thompson</dc:creator>
      <pubDate>Thu, 28 May 2026 20:22:27 +0000</pubDate>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Artificial Intelligence</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Real Estate and Housing (Residential)</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Real Estate Brokers</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_geo">Hudson River Valley (NY)</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Appraisals and Valuation (Property)</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Regulation and Deregulation of Industry</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Computers and the Internet</category>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>Anthropic Tops OpenAI to Become the World’s Most Valuable A.I. Start-Up</title>
      <link>https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/technology/anthropic-tops-openai-valuation.html</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/technology/anthropic-tops-openai-valuation.html</guid>
      <atom:link href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/technology/anthropic-tops-openai-valuation.html" rel="standout"></atom:link>
      <description>Anthropic raised $65 billion in new fund-raising that put its value at $900 billion, ahead of OpenAI’s last valuation of $730 billion, as the companies duel for A.I. dominance.</description>
      <dc:creator>Mike Isaac and Cade Metz</dc:creator>
      <pubDate>Thu, 28 May 2026 19:50:31 +0000</pubDate>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">Anthropic AI LLC</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">OpenAI Labs</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Artificial Intelligence</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Computers and the Internet</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Venture Capital</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Start-ups</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">internal-open-access-from-nl</category>
      <media:content height="1800" medium="image" url="https://static01.nyt.com/images/2026/05/28/multimedia/28biz-anthropic-coding-kfmq/28biz-anthropic-coding-kfmq-mediumSquareAt3X.jpg" width="1800"></media:content>
      <media:credit>Jason Henry for The New York Times</media:credit>
      <media:description>Anthropic’s updated model, Claude Opus 4.8, is particularly adept at vibecoding, or the process of artificial intelligence writing code from prompts in conversational English.</media:description>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>A.I. Is Making Scams Hard to Spot. Here’s How to Protect Yourself.</title>
      <link>https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/technology/personaltech/scams-ai.html</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/technology/personaltech/scams-ai.html</guid>
      <atom:link href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/technology/personaltech/scams-ai.html" rel="standout"></atom:link>
      <description>A criminal could be masquerading as a celebrity, web store or family member asking for your money. Detecting scams requires a new approach.</description>
      <dc:creator>Brian X. Chen</dc:creator>
      <pubDate>Thu, 28 May 2026 15:54:54 +0000</pubDate>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Frauds and Swindling</category>

      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">Meta Platforms Inc</category>
      <media:content height="1800" medium="image" url="https://static01.nyt.com/images/2026/05/29/business/28techfix-print/28techfix-mediumSquareAt3X.jpg" width="1800"></media:content>
      <media:credit>Derek Abella</media:credit>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>In Argentina, U.S. Tech Billionaire Peter Thiel Finds An Escape</title>
      <link>https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/world/americas/peter-thiel-argentina.html</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/world/americas/peter-thiel-argentina.html</guid>
      <atom:link href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/28/world/americas/peter-thiel-argentina.html" rel="standout"></atom:link>
      <description>The billionaire’s new roots in Argentina are said to be partly motivated by concerns about the future of the United States and shared beliefs with Argentina’s right-wing leader.</description>
      <dc:creator>Emma Bubola and Ryan Mac</dc:creator>
      <pubDate>Thu, 28 May 2026 12:22:29 +0000</pubDate>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Thiel, Peter A</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">High Net Worth Individuals</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Computers and the Internet</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Venture Capital</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_geo">Argentina</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Milei, Javier</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Politics and Government</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">United States Politics and Government</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">United States International Relations</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Trump, Donald J</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Content Type: Personal Profile</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">internal-open-access-from-nl</category>
      <media:content height="1800" medium="image" url="https://static01.nyt.com/images/2026/05/26/multimedia/00int-argentina-thiel01-photo-tfzh/00int-argentina-thiel01-photo-tfzh-mediumSquareAt3X.jpg" width="1800"></media:content>
      <media:credit>Matias Baglietto/Reuters</media:credit>
      <media:description>Peter Thiel, right, arriving for a meeting with President Javier Milei at the Casa Rosada Presidential Palace in Buenos Aires in April.</media:description>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>Powerful A.I. Super PACs Duel Over the Midterms: ‘This Is a War’</title>
      <link>https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/30/us/politics/anthropic-openai-super-pacs-midterms.html</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/30/us/politics/anthropic-openai-super-pacs-midterms.html</guid>
      <atom:link href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/30/us/politics/anthropic-openai-super-pacs-midterms.html" rel="standout"></atom:link>
      <description>One super PAC is allied with Anthropic. The other is tied to OpenAI. They’re both spending millions to influence this year’s elections.</description>
      <dc:creator>Theodore Schleifer</dc:creator>
      <pubDate>Sat, 30 May 2026 14:09:34 +0000</pubDate>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Artificial Intelligence</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Political Action Committees</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Midterm Elections (2026)</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Lobbying and Lobbyists</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Regulation and Deregulation of Industry</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Campaign Finance</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Political Advertising</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">United States Politics and Government</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">Anthropic AI LLC</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">OpenAI Labs</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Altman, Samuel H</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Amodei, Dario</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Bores, Alex</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Brockman, Greg</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Foushee, Valerie P</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Jackson, Jesse L Jr</category>
      <media:content height="1800" medium="image" url="https://static01.nyt.com/images/2026/05/30/multimedia/30pol-ai-money-duel-twjl/30pol-ai-money-duel-twjl-mediumSquareAt3X.jpg" width="1800"></media:content>
      <media:credit>Kenny Holston/The New York Times</media:credit>
      <media:description>The artificial intelligence industry has begun spending tens of millions of dollars on elections, routing the money through allied super PACs. </media:description>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>What’s Next for Blue Origin After Rocket Explosion</title>
      <link>https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/29/technology/blue-origin-amazon-rocket-explosion.html</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/29/technology/blue-origin-amazon-rocket-explosion.html</guid>
      <atom:link href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/29/technology/blue-origin-amazon-rocket-explosion.html" rel="standout"></atom:link>
      <description>Jeff Bezos was gaining ground on Elon Musk’s SpaceX and Starlink. Thursday’s rocket explosion on a launchpad creates a major setback.</description>
      <dc:creator>Karen Weise</dc:creator>
      <pubDate>Fri, 29 May 2026 21:51:12 +0000</pubDate>

      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">Space Exploration Technologies Corp</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">Starlink Satellite Constellation (SpaceX)</category>

      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_geo">Cape Canaveral (Fla)</category>
      <media:content height="1797" medium="image" url="https://static01.nyt.com/images/2026/05/29/multimedia/29biz-blue-origin-1-hcpz/29biz-blue-origin-1-hcpz-mediumSquareAt3X.jpg" width="1800"></media:content>
      <media:credit>Nasaspaceflight.com, via Reuters</media:credit>
      <media:description>Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket exploded on its launchpad during a test.</media:description>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>Interesting Times: Why Are We Still Driving?</title>
      <link>https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/29/podcasts/29hardfork-waymo-interesting-times.html</link>

    <item>
      <title>Teachers Union Urges Schools to Curb A.I. Chatbots and Screen Time</title>
      <link>https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/27/technology/ai-screens-schools-weingarten.html</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/27/technology/ai-screens-schools-weingarten.html</guid>
      <atom:link href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/27/technology/ai-screens-schools-weingarten.html" rel="standout"></atom:link>
      <description>The American Federation of Teachers recommended “no screens” at all for those in second grade or younger, and no A.I. chatbots for students in elementary school.</description>
      <dc:creator>Natasha Singer</dc:creator>
      <pubDate>Wed, 27 May 2026 12:58:05 +0000</pubDate>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Education (K-12)</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Weingarten, Randi</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">American Federation of Teachers</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Teachers and School Employees</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Organized Labor</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Artificial Intelligence</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">OpenAI Labs</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Screen Time (Device)</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Computers and the Internet</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_geo">United States</category>
      <media:content height="1800" medium="image" url="https://static01.nyt.com/images/2026/05/27/multimedia/27Biz-AI-Weingarten-1-pzkm/27Biz-AI-Weingarten-1-pzkm-mediumSquareAt3X.jpg" width="1800"></media:content>
      <media:credit>Eric Lee/The New York Times</media:credit>
      <media:description>Randi Weingarten, president of the American Federation of Teachers, said on Wednesday that teaching and learning in the earliest grades “should be done without A.I.”</media:description>
    </item>
    <item>

    <item>
      <title>At the A.I. Epicenter, Technologists Dismiss Pope Leo’s Warnings About the New Technology</title>

    <item>
      <title>SpaceX IPO Filing Reveals Favorable Terms for Elon Musk</title>
      <link>https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/26/technology/spacex-elon-musk-pay-board-governance.html</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/26/technology/spacex-elon-musk-pay-board-governance.html</guid>
      <atom:link href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/26/technology/spacex-elon-musk-pay-board-governance.html" rel="standout"></atom:link>
      <description>The ways it set up its board and Mr. Musk’s pay appear to benefit him at the expense of other shareholders, corporate governance experts said.</description>
      <dc:creator>Ryan Mac</dc:creator>
      <pubDate>Tue, 26 May 2026 18:59:37 +0000</pubDate>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">Space Exploration Technologies Corp</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Musk, Elon</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">Tesla Motors Inc</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Boards of Directors</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Stocks and Bonds</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Private Spaceflight</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Executive Compensation</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Space and Astronomy</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Arbitration, Conciliation and Mediation</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Corporations</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Computers and the Internet</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">Starlink Satellite Constellation (SpaceX)</category>

    <item>
      <title>Sundar Pichai Understands Why People Are Anxious About A.I.</title>
      <link>https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/22/podcasts/sundar-pichai-understands-why-people-are-anxious-about-ai.html</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/22/podcasts/sundar-pichai-understands-why-people-are-anxious-about-ai.html</guid>
      <atom:link href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/22/podcasts/sundar-pichai-understands-why-people-are-anxious-about-ai.html" rel="standout"></atom:link>
      <description>After a busy Google I/O, the company’s chief executive sits down with the hosts of “Hard Fork” to discuss the future of Google Search, how he’s using A.I. agents and his advice for college graduates.</description>
      <dc:creator>Kevin Roose, Casey Newton and Whitney Jones</dc:creator>
      <pubDate>Fri, 22 May 2026 14:49:56 +0000</pubDate>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Artificial Intelligence</category>
      <category domain="">Gemini (Google)</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Computers and the Internet</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/des">Search Engines</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_per">Pichai, Sundar</category>
      <category domain="http://www.nytimes.com/namespaces/keywords/nyt_org">Google Inc</category>
      <media:content height="1800" medium="image" url="https://static01.nyt.com/images/2026/05/22/multimedia/22hardfork-sundar/22hardfork-sundar-mediumSquareAt3X.jpg" width="1800"></media:content>
      <media:credit>Vjeran Pavic/The New York Times</media:credit>
      <media:description>“People, rightfully so, are anxious about the future that this technology will bring,” Google’s chief executive, Sundar Pichai, said this week after the company’s annual developer conference.</media:description>
    </item>
